c74object.name: dk.gridmatch
c74object.digest: Find the nearest match in a pre-analyzed corpus
c74object.description: The dk.gridmatch object finds the nearest match in a corpus based on a grid-ified XY space. You can load/use the same corpora as you would with dk.corpusmatch but instead of matching based on incoming audio descriptors you can match using XY coordinates from a controller or UI object.
c74object.discussion:
If you want to learn more about the underlying algorithms have a look at these articles:
https://learn.flucoma.org/reference/kdtree
https://learn.flucoma.org/reference/mlpregressor

outlet.digest: Dict output for dk.plotter or dk.eraetouch
outlet.description: Dict output for dk.plotter or dk.eraetouch.

outlet.digest: Waveform and slice display for fluid.waveform~
outlet.description: Waveform and slice display for fluid.waveform~.

method.name: dump
method.digest: Dumps out the current entries as a list
method.description: Dumps out current entries as a list (filtered or otherwise) as a list.

method.name: dumpdict
method.digest: Dumps out the current entries as a list
method.description: Dumps out current entries as a list (filtered or otherwise) as a dictionary.

method.name: info
method.digest: Print corpus metatdata/info to Max console
method.description: Prints a summary of the corpus metatdata/info to the Max console.

method.name: read
method.description: Reads a pre-trained concat .json file directly from a relative or absolute path. With no additional argument, a file dialog will appear to choose a .json file.

method.name: print
method.digest: Print dataset contents to Max console
method.description: Prints a summary of the descriptors in the dataset to the Max console.

attribute.name: active
attribute.digest: Enables or disables the matching
attribute.description: Enables or disables the matching.

attribute.name: corpus
attribute.digest: Messages related to corpus matching
attribute.description:
Messages related to corpus matching.
After sending the corpus message you send an argument of 'match' followed by a time-scale you wish to match against.
The available options are:
short = the first 256 samples (at 44.1k sample rate)
medium = the first 4410 samples (at 44.1k sample rate)
all = the entire file
Therefore a complete message would look as follows:
corpus match all or corpus match small

attribute.name: fastrampdown
attribute.digest: The number of samples the relative envelope follower will take to reach the next value when falling
attribute.description: The number of samples the relative envelope follower will take to reach the next value when falling. Typically, this will be faster than slowrampdown.

attribute.name: fastrampup
attribute.digest: The number of samples the relative envelope follower will take to reach the next value when raising
attribute.description: The number of samples the relative envelope follower will take to reach the next value when raising. Typically, this will be faster than slowrampup.

attribute.name: floor
attribute.digest: The level in dB the slow ramp needs to be above to consider a detected difference valid, allowing to ignore the onsets in the noise floor
attribute.description: The level in dB the slow ramp needs to be above to consider a detected difference valid, allowing to ignore the onsets in the noise floor.

attribute.name: highpassfreq
attribute.digest: The frequency of the fourth-order high-pass filter. This is done first on the signal to minimise low frequency intermodulation with very fast ramp lengths.
attribute.description: The frequency of the fourth-order Linkwitz–Riley high-pass filter (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Linkwitz%E2%80%93Riley_filter). This is done first on the signal to minimise low frequency intermodulation with very fast ramp lengths. A frequency of 0 bypasses the filter.

attribute.name: iterations
attribute.digest: The number of iterations that the algorithm will go through
attribute.description: The number of iterations that the algorithm will go through to optimise the new representation (minimum 1).

attribute.name: learnrate
attribute.digest: Learning rate of the algorithm
attribute.description: The learning rate of the algorithm, aka how much of the error it uses to estimate the next iteration.

attribute.name: lockout
attribute.digest: Amount of time (in ms) before it can get triggered again
attribute.description: Amount of time (in ms) before an onset can get triggered again.

attribute.name: matchcount
attribute.digest: Sets the number of matches that are returned
attribute.description: Sets the number of matches that are returned as iterated items, which will be played back as a "chord".

attribute.name: mindist
attribute.digest: Minimum distance each point is allowed to be from the others in the low dimension space
attribute.description: The minimum distance each point is allowed to be from the others in the low dimension space. Low values will make tighter clumps, and higher will spread the points more.

attribute.name: minslicelength
attribute.digest: The length in samples that the slice will stay ON
attribute.description: The length in samples that the Slice will stay ON. Changes of states during that period will be ignored. This is the equivalent of lockout but set in samples.

attribute.name: numneighbors
attribute.digest: Number of neighbors considered by the algorithm to balance local vs global structures
attribute.description: The number of neighbors considered by the algorithm to balance local vs global structures to conserve. Low values will prioritise preservation of the local structure while high values will prioritise preservation of the global structure.

attribute.name: offthreshold
attribute.digest: The threshold in dB of the relative envelope follower to reset, aka to allow the differential envelope to trigger again.
attribute.description: The threshold in dB of the relative envelope follower to reset, aka to allow the differential envelope to trigger again.

attribute.name: onthreshold
attribute.digest: The threshold in dB of the relative envelope follower to trigger an onset, aka to go ON when in OFF state
attribute.description: The threshold in dB of the relative envelope follower to trigger an onset, aka to go ON when in OFF state. It is computed on the difference between the two envelope followers.

attribute.name: roundrobin
attribute.digest: Enable roundrobin sample matching
attribute.description:
Enables round robin sample matching so when a match is found twice in a row, a randomized selection is made within the nearest matches.
The available options are:
0 = round robin selection is off
1 = round robin selection is on (default 5 nearest neighbors)

attribute.name: refer
attribute.digest: Messages related to buffer~ loading
attribute.description:
Messages related to buffer~ loading and referring.
You can point the analysis to an existing buffer~ by sending sending a refer message followed by a valid buffer~ name. This will immediately analyze the named buffer~ and make it available for playback as soon as the process is done.
Additional optional arguments allow you to specify a subsection of the buffer~ to load for analysis.
A single additional argument will specify how much of the buffer~ (in ms) to analyze from the start. If two arguments are given these will specify the start position (in ms) of the analysis and then the duration (in ms) of the buffer~ to analyze after that point.
Therefore a complete message would look as follows:
refer bufferName or refer bufferName 500 or refer bufferName 500 1500

attribute.name: sensitivity
attribute.digest: The nervousness of the algorithm thresholds (from 0 to 100%)
attribute.description: The nervousness of the algorithm thresholds (from 0 to 100%). This is essentially a 'macro' parameter for the individual on/off thresholds.

attribute.name: slowrampdown
attribute.digest: The number of samples the absolute envelope follower will take to reach the next value when falling.
attribute.description: The number of samples the absolute envelope follower will take to reach the next value when falling.

attribute.name: slowrampup
attribute.digest: The number of samples the absolute envelope follower will take to reach the next value when raising.
attribute.description: The number of samples the absolute envelope follower will take to reach the next value when raising.
